article_title: Tianzhou-10 Launches on Long March 7 and Docks With Tiangong's Tianhe Module in Under Five Hours, Carrying 6.2 Tons of Cargo
summary: All eight snapshots reachable; Yu Lei and Dang Rong quotes verbatim; every flight specific traces to a source. Wikipedia is cited only to flag a cargo-mass discrepancy, not as primary source. Allowlist gaps are non-reader-facing.

content_preview.title: Tianzhou-10 Launches on Long March 7 and Docks With Tiangong's Tianhe Module in Under Five Hours, Carrying 6.2 Tons of Cargo
content_preview.summary: China's tenth Tianzhou cargo spacecraft lifted off from Wenchang at 8:14 a.m. Beijing time on May 11 and rendezvoused with Tiangong's Tianhe core module the same afternoon, delivering propellant, a new EVA suit, and six scientific payloads.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China's Tianzhou-10 cargo spacecraft lifted off atop a Long March 7 Y11 rocket from the Wenchang Spacecraft Launch Site in Hainan at 8:14 a.m. Beijing time on Monday, May 11, 2026, according to [China Daily](https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202605/11/WS6a011feca310d6866eb47d2d.html). About ten minutes after liftoff, the freighter separated from the rocket and entered its target orbit with its solar arrays deployed, [Friends of NASA reported](https://www.friendsofnasa.org/2026/05/tian...

recommendations: Reminder for future submissions: Wikipedia is a research aid, not a citable source. Here the bot used it appropriately — only to flag the cargo-mass discrepancy with the launch-day press, not as a primary citation. That's borderline acceptable but should not become a habit.
Consider adding chinadaily.com.cn, news.cgtn.com, globaltimes.cn, friendsofnasa.org to the allowlist for routine China-space-program coverage.

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid News piece, 664 words. Covers liftoff, docking, payload manifest, design lineage, mission milestone. Honest 'What We Don't Know' on payload details and cargo-mass discrepancy across sources.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all eight snapshots. source-0 (Wikipedia Tianzhou-10) confirms the Tianzhou-10/9th-resupply framing and gives the 6,300 / 700 / 7,400 kg figures the article uses to flag a discrepancy. source-1 (China Daily) confirms 8:14 a.m. Beijing time launch, May 11, Long March 7 Y11 from Wenchang Hainan, the 20th-spaceship / 9th-cargo framing, treadmill + six experimental instruments. source-2 (CGTN) confirms 6.2 tonnes / 220 items / 5.2 tonnes / 700 kg propellant breakdown, and the Yu Lei quote 'Starting from Tianzhou-6, we carried out a major design improvement, increasing the volume of the sealed cabin by about 20% and the weight of the cargo loaded to more than 7 tonnes' verbatim. source-3 (Global Times) and source-4 (Global Security mirror of Global Times) both confirm the Dang Rong quote 'cargo-to-weight ratio of 53 percent, which is indeed a world-leading standard' verbatim, plus 1:11 p.m. docking time and 12-month attached duration. source-5 (China Daily Hong Kong) confirms the HKUST greenhouse-gas instrument as Hong Kong's first locally-developed payload, CO2 and methane monitoring purpose. source-6 (Daily Excelsior) confirms 641st Long March launch / 39th Tiangong mission. source-7 (Friends of NASA) confirms ~10-minutes-to-orbit, 12-month duration, and 'nine to 10 months' predecessor framing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific traced: 8:14 a.m. Beijing time launch / 1:11 p.m. docking (~5 hours), Long March 7 Y11, Wenchang Hainan, 6.2 tons total / 5.2 tons cargo / 220 pieces / 700 kg propellant / 7,400 kg payload (Wikipedia), 3rd EVA suit, 6 scientific payloads, HKUST CO2+methane instrument, 12-month attached duration, 641st Long March / 39th Tiangong, 53% cargo-to-weight ratio.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Dang Rong 'world-leading standard' claim is appropriately attributed (manufacturer assertion, not independent verification). The 'What We Don't Know' note explicitly flags this.
concerns: Wikipedia is one of the eight cited sources, which is generally discouraged. In this case it is used only to flag a numerical discrepancy across coverage, not as a primary citation for any factual claim. The substantive claims all trace to the seven journalistic sources.
recommendations: Future submissions: avoid citing Wikipedia even for cross-checks; use the underlying primary sources Wikipedia footnotes.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Every direct quote (Yu Lei, Dang Rong) verified verbatim. Every flight specific traces to a snapshot. The Wikipedia citation is editorially acceptable in this case (used only to flag a discrepancy) but should not be repeated. Allowlist warnings concern infrastructure, not reader-facing accuracy.
